title: Build with yarn dependency error
body: ## Description This pull request resolves a Netlify build failure stemming from a `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` error for `yarn-1.22.22.cjs`. The issue occurred because Netlify's Node.js 20 environment, with Corepack enabled, attempted to use a project-local Yarn Berry shim that was not present, while the project intended to use Yarn Classic. The fix involves: 1. Removing the `packageManager` field from `package.json` to prevent Corepack from misinterpreting the Yarn version. 2. Updating the Netlify build command in `netlify.toml` to explicitly disable Corepack and globally install Yarn Classic (v1.22.22) before running `yarn install`. This ensures the build uses the correct Yarn version without expecting a non-existent local shim. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - title: CodeQL analyze javascript typescript
body: ## Description This pull request resolves the consistent failure of the CodeQL / Analyze (javascript-typescript) job. The failures stemmed from two main issues: 1. **Missing Node.js environment and dependencies:** The CodeQL autobuild step was failing because Node.js and project dependencies were not installed prior to analysis. body: ## Description This pull request implements a series of enhancements focused on improving the application's SEO, performance, and security, alongside modernizing the build process. Key changes include: - **SEO Optimization**: Integrated `react-helmet-async` for dynamic meta tag management, replacing the previous `SEO` component with `EnhancedSEO` and providing specific title, description, and canonical tags. - **Build Performance**: Configured Vite to use `vite-plugin-compression` for gzip and brotli compression, disabled sourcemaps for production builds, and set the build target to `es2018`. A root `index.html` was added for Vite's build process. - **Security Hardening**: Updated `public/_headers` to enforce stricter HSTS, expanded the Permissions-Policy, and implemented aggressive caching for static assets like fonts and images. - **Dependency Management**: Upgraded TypeScript to v5 to resolve peer dependency conflicts. - **Code Structure**: Refactored `BrowserRouter` to wrap the application in `src/main.tsx` alongside `HelmetProvider` for correct context provisioning. title: Analyze, improve, and deploy ziontechgroup app
body: ## Description This PR addresses critical build configuration issues to enable successful compilation of the application using Vite. 1. **Vite Build Input Resolution:** The project was missing a root `index.html` file, which is essential for Vite to correctly identify the application's entry point. A new `index.html` has been added to the project root, configured to point to `src/main.tsx`. 2. **TypeScript Peer Dependency Conflict:** An outdated TypeScript version (`^4.9.5`) was causing peer dependency conflicts, particularly with `react-i18next`. TypeScript has been upgraded to `^5.6.3` to resolve these conflicts and ensure compatibility.
